title: Chuquiraga spinosa
taxonomy: Chuquiraga spinosa is classified as a flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, which is commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It is a genus of shrubs that are native to South America, particularly in the Andes mountain range. The species Chuquiraga spinosa is a type of flowering shrub that belongs to the family Asteraceae. The plant is known for its spiny branches and vibrant yellow flowers. Chuquiraga spinosa is often found growing in dry, rocky areas at high elevations.

The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, gastrointestinal issues, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties.
